criteria.name: Identification of Key Variables
criteria.description: Student correctly identifies and defines the key variables relevant to the supply and demand problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All relevant variables are identified and clearly defined, showing a deep understanding of their roles in the market.
criteria.poor_indicator: Key variables are either missing or poorly defined, leading to confusion about their relevance.

criteria.name: Articulation of the Problem Context
criteria.description: Student effectively describes the context and background of the problem, including market conditions and stakeholder impacts.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The context is thoroughly articulated, providing a clear picture of the market dynamics involved.
criteria.poor_indicator: The context is vague or incomplete, lacking essential details that affect the understanding of the problem.

criteria.name: Clarity of the Well-Defined Task
criteria.description: Student translates the ill-defined problem into a clear, actionable task that can be analyzed using microeconomic principles.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The task is well-structured and explicitly aligns with microeconomic analysis methods and terminology.
criteria.poor_indicator: The task is unclear or misaligned with microeconomic analysis, making it difficult to approach systematically.

typical_errors: Failing to identify all key variables affecting supply and demand.
Describing the context in overly simplistic terms without considering market complexities.
Formulating a task that lacks specificity or measurable outcomes.

criteria.name: Evaluation of AI Output Accuracy
criteria.description: Student assesses the accuracy of AI-generated responses regarding economic principles and data.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All inaccuracies are identified with specific examples and explanations of why they are incorrect.
criteria.poor_indicator: Few inaccuracies are identified, or the student fails to explain why the AI output is inaccurate.

criteria.name: Identification of Economic Concepts
criteria.description: Student identifies and critiques the economic concepts applied in the AI output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Relevant economic concepts are accurately identified and explained in relation to the problem at hand.
criteria.poor_indicator: Economic concepts are misunderstood or misapplied, leading to incorrect evaluations of the AI output.

criteria.name: Critical Analysis of Assumptions
criteria.description: Student critically examines the assumptions made by the AI in its responses.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All assumptions are analyzed in-depth, considering their validity and impact on the conclusions drawn.
criteria.poor_indicator: Assumptions are either overlooked or inadequately analyzed, leading to flawed judgments.

typical_errors: Overlooking critical assumptions made by the AI that affect the analysis.
Failing to recognize misapplications of economic concepts in the output.
Not providing sufficient evidence or examples to support judgments of accuracy.

criteria.name: Clarity of Correction Commands
criteria.description: Student provides clear and specific instructions to correct the AI output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Corrections are detailed and directly address identified inaccuracies with precise terminology.
criteria.poor_indicator: Corrections are vague or lack specificity, making it unclear how to improve the AI output.

criteria.name: Relevance of Suggested Changes
criteria.description: Student suggests changes that are relevant to the identified issues in the AI output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All suggested changes are pertinent and grounded in microeconomic theory, enhancing the accuracy of the output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Suggested changes are irrelevant or do not address the identified issues effectively.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement Process
criteria.description: Student demonstrates a systematic approach to iteratively refining the AI output based on feedback.
criteria.excellent_indicator: A clear iterative process is evident, with each round of corrections building on previous feedback effectively.
criteria.poor_indicator: The approach lacks structure, with corrections seeming random or disconnected from prior feedback.

typical_errors: Making vague or general correction commands that do not specify the issues.
Suggesting changes that do not directly relate to the identified inaccuracies in the AI output.
Failing to follow an iterative process, leading to incomplete or ineffective corrections.
